mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly increase in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly jump in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movements between housing starts and retail sales credit indicate conflicting demand signals; when housing starts decline while retail sales credit expands, equity valuations contract due to earnings uncertainty, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.10) for 60-day forward returns, or the signal generates positive returns in the divergence regime more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production exceeds its 12-month moving average, supply-side bottlenecks amplify inflation expectations, forcing higher discount rates that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO peaks above trend and CPIAUCSL accelerates, or the regime-specific IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential monthly increase in retail sales credit reflects strengthening consumer spending momentum, which directly supports near-term corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 2-year out-of-sample test, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between rising and falling credit periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 1.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to discount long-duration equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, or the signal's predictive rank correlation remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, reducing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline PAYEMS-only signal in out-of-sample tests, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both series move favorably (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase characterized by rapidly rising housing starts, construction-related earnings revisions accelerate, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when HOUST rises above its 3-month average during a post-shock recovery, or the regime-specific IC remains below 0.03 over a 2-year window.
